title: Ketan Agrawal murder: Birthday plot, secret lover and a detail that exposed fiancee's story
content: A tragic trek to Lohagad Fort has culminated in a murder investigation, with police arresting a woman and her partner for allegedly pushing her fiancé, Ketan Agrawal, to his death. The couple reportedly conspired to eliminate Agrawal as the fiancée was unwilling to marry him. Initially ruled an accident, the skilled trekker's family suspected foul play, leading to the unraveling of the shocking plot.

title: Dhurandhars or traitors: How Shinde’s 'Op Tiger' has changed Maharashtra’s power dynamics
content: Maharashtra's political scene is rapidly changing, as the recent defection of six Uddhav Sena MPs to Eknath Shinde's faction under 'Operation Tiger' highlights. This trend reflects a broader pattern seen in Trinamool Congress and AAP, where the anti-defection law is being sidestepped, and parties blatantly promote shifts in loyalty. The fear of the Samajwadi Party and the NCP being the next targets in this game of defections shows the vulnerability of regional parties in this political maneuvering.

title: How hydration breaks are reshaping matches at the 2026 FIFA World Cup
content: FIFA’s mandatory hydration breaks at the World Cup, under the guise of player welfare, have sparked debate. While introduced for all matches regardless of weather and conditions, coaches and players argue they disrupt momentum and alter football’s rhythm. Beyond altering the game, the breaks also create significant advertising inventory for broadcasters with Fox Sports in the USA expected to rake in $250 million. At the end of the day, do the commercial interests outweigh the sporting considerations?

title: Iran rushes to sell oil to India after Trump sanctions waiver - all you need to know
content: Data from Vortexa, combined with Bloomberg calculations, show that around 68 million barrels of crude and condensate were floating at sea as of June 22. More than 80% of that volume does not appear to have a confirmed destination, potentially making it available for sale.

title: Oracle layoffs: Company confirms how many employees got 6AM email from leadership
content: Oracle has initiated significant layoffs globally, impacting thousands of employees across US, India, and other regions.  These workforce reductions, attributed to management changes, AI adoption, and strategic shifts, resulted in a 13% drop in its workforce.  The company incurred substantial restructuring costs, with severance packages reportedly less generous than industry peers.  This move comes amidst broader concerns about AI's impact on tech jobs.

title: 'Attempt to deflect': India snubs Khawaja Asif's 'will go to war over Indus' remark
content: MEA spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal also spoke on the ongoing protest in the Pakistan Occupied Kashmir (PoK), saying the agitation was a  "direct consequence" of "systemic economic exploitation, denial of fundamental rights and administrative oppression in areas under its illegal and forcible occupation".​​ Earlier, Khawaja Asif had issued a warning to India, saying Islamabad could resort to military action if its water security comes under threat.

title: India–US trade deal may be signed before July 24: What’s done and remaining for agreement
content: India and the US are nearing a trade deal, with negotiators meeting in New Delhi to finalize an interim pact before July 24. Discussions aim to recalibrate the agreement following recent US tariff policy shifts. Both nations are pushing for a fair and reciprocal deal that benefits American exporters and supports India's growth, potentially involving significant Indian purchases from the US.

title: Message seen on WhatsApp: Kunal Shah gets real CRED from Meta
content: Kunal Shah is an interesting choice, because WhatsApp does not need a leader to teach people how to send a message; it needs one who understands how trust, status, habit, and small transactions mutate inside closed networks, which is, almost exactly, the discipline CRED was built to practise.

title: After 43 yrs, US family sells electrical firm for $1.7bn, makes 540 workers millionaires
content: A small-town US family has sold their Louisiana-based electrical-equipment company Fibrebond to power-management giant Eaton for $1.7 billion—and made sure their 540 full-time workers got a $240 million cut of it. Former CEO Graham Walker wrote a single clause into the deal: 15% of proceeds for the staff, none of whom held any equity. The average bonus works out to around $443,000 per worker.

title: Meet Kunal Shah: From delivery boy at 15 to WhatsApp’s global head
content: Kunal Shah, the visionary behind CRED, is set to lead WhatsApp globally, marking a significant milestone for Indian tech talent.  His journey from humble beginnings in Mumbai, marked by early entrepreneurship and a unique approach to education, highlights a remarkable rise.  Shah's success underscores the power of merit and innovation in the competitive tech landscape, offering inspiration to aspiring professionals worldwide.

title: After laying off hundreds of engineers, GM now replaces more than 1,000 workers with 50 robots
content: General Motors has introduced 50 collaborative robots at its Michigan Factory Zero, reportedly leading to over 1,000 job eliminations. This move, aimed at cost-cutting and efficiency, sparks criticism from the United Auto Workers union, which views it as a threat to jobs. GM claims the robots support human workers, but the UAW president calls it a "fight for humanity" amid broader automation concerns in the industry.

title: Ukraine's 'army of witches' helping hunt Russian troops in occupied lands — Report
content: The intelligence collected by these operatives has become a key component of Ukraine's so-called "middle-strike" campaign, a sustained drone offensive targeting Russian command posts, logistics hubs, air-defence systems and troop concentrations behind the front line. Resistance members identify locations, verify coordinates and relay information through encrypted channels before Ukrainian forces launch strikes.

title: Henry David Thoreau’s quote on self-belief: A timeless lesson in shaping destiny
content: Henry David Thoreau, a 19th-century philosopher and naturalist, championed self-reliance and living by one's own values. His time at Walden Pond inspired his seminal work, urging readers to simplify life and connect with nature. Thoreau's belief that self-perception shapes destiny, as highlighted in his quote, remains profoundly relevant, reminding us that inner attitudes often dictate our path more than external circumstances.

title: Tom Brady sends clear message after Gisele’s alleged Father’s Day snub
content: Tom Brady, the seven time Super Bowl champion, has had a pretty successful career but his past marriage with ex wife, Gisele Bundchen, has continued to dominate headlines even after years of their divorce. The Brazilian supermodel Gisele Bundchen recently made a controversial post for Father’s Day and it seems the NFL legend Tom Brady has made it clear what his current priorities are.

title: New Champions crowned, shocking betrayal headlines explosive WWE Raw Night
content: London's O2 Arena witnessed a dramatic WWE RAW go-home show for Night of Champions.  A beloved star turned heel, shocking fans, while Chad Gable faced his former stablemates.  Key bouts saw LA Knight triumph over Jimmy Uso amidst Bloodline interference, and The Street Profits capture the WWE World Tag Team Titles in a chaotic main event, culminating in a surprise championship coronation.

title: Who is Geno Smith’s alleged Ex? Inside the claims stirring NFL attention
content: The New York Jets’ star player, Geno Smith, was gearing up for the next season when he found himself in major legal trouble after an alleged ex girlfriend accused him of domestic violence and being neglectful of a child just a few hours ago. The controversial accusations have sparked shockwaves across the league and many are curious about Geno Smith’s alleged ex girlfriend.

title: 'His body is fragile': Ex-India star raises concern over Hardik Pandya's 2027 WC chances
content: Former India cricketer Mohammad Kaif expressed concern over Hardik Pandya's persistent fitness issues, highlighting a lack of transparency regarding his injuries. Kaif questioned Pandya's long-term availability for future tournaments, including the 2027 ODI World Cup.  Meanwhile, Kaif supported the selectors' decision to pick Ishan Kishan over Yashasvi Jaiswal for the England ODI series, citing Kishan's versatility and current form.

title: Who has suffered the biggest pay cut in IPL history as Pant joins DC?
content: Rishabh Pant's return to Delhi Capitals after a disappointing two-year stint with Lucknow Super Giants came with a Rs 12 crore pay cut, from Rs 27 crore to Rs 15 crore. His move highlights how IPL valuations can change dramatically. Other stars who suffered major pay drops include Venkatesh Iyer, Sam Curran, Mitchell Starc, Kyle Jamieson and Jhye Richardson.

title: The $750 billion question: Report claims China fudged trade data to fool the IMF
content: According to the Council on Foreign Relations, the comparison relies on incomplete data and fails to capture the sharp rise in China's surplus since late 2024. It notes that China's reported current account surplus increased from about $400 billion to roughly $750 billion, while net exports contributed more than 1.5 percentage points to economic growth. During the same period, the eurozone's surplus fell from around 420 billion euros to 280 billion euros.

title: First ‘cell rejuvenation’ therapy in humans: Can ageing finally be treated?
content: The innovative cell rejuvenation therapy, known as ER-100, is set to enter human trials, focused on enhancing the functionality of aging cells rather than attempting to reverse the aging process. This revolutionary approach shows promise in potentially decelerating age-related decline and addressing conditions like Alzheimer's. However, medical professionals advise caution due to ongoing research into long-term safety and efficacy, indicating a shift in understanding aging as a fixed outcome.

title: Govinda’s wife Sunita Ahuja quits 'Maa Hai Na', cites ailing mother’s health
content: Bollywood actress Shilpa Shetty Kundra's show 'Maa Hai Na' witnessed an emotional departure as Govinda's wife Sunita Ahuja and daughter Tina Ahuja exited midway. Sunita revealed her mother's ill health as the reason, prioritizing family above the show. She expressed deep gratitude for the experience, highlighting how it strengthened her bond with Tina and reaffirmed her daughter's love.

title: Lucknow fire deaths: How smoke, toxic gases and oxygen loss became bigger killers than flames
content: Doctors highlight that smoke inhalation and toxic gases, not just burns, are the primary killers in fires, especially in enclosed spaces.  These invisible hazards rapidly reduce oxygen and introduce deadly fumes, leading to suffocation and internal injuries.  While burns pose significant risks to survivors, the immediate danger often comes from the air itself, making awareness crucial for preventing fatalities.

title: Trisha lovingly looks at Vijay in late birthday post for him amid unfollowing speculations
content: Fans are buzzing as Trisha Krishnan broke her tradition of posting birthday wishes for Vijay this year. Her usual heartfelt messages and candid photos were absent on his 52nd birthday, sparking speculation online. While some claim she unfollowed him on Instagram, this remains unconfirmed. The beloved on-screen pair, known for hits like 'Leo', has been subject to personal equation rumors, though neither has commented.

title: Swine Flu isn’t always mild: H1N1 death highlights who is most at risk and the warning signs
content: A recent H1N1 death in Karnataka underscores that swine flu remains a serious threat beyond seasonal illness. Spreading through respiratory droplets and contaminated surfaces, it presents flu-like symptoms but can lead to severe complications, especially for vulnerable groups like the elderly, young children, and those with chronic conditions. Doctors stress early recognition, timely treatment, and vaccination as crucial defenses against potentially life-threatening outcomes.

title: After Pakistan, now Bangladesh eyes China’s J-10CE fighter: Why it matters
content: Bangladesh is reportedly set to acquire 24 Chinese J-10CE fighter jets, a significant move for both nations. This deal, if finalized, would mark the second export for China's advanced fighter, following Pakistan's acquisition. The procurement aims to bolster the Bangladeshi Air Force's combat capabilities, replacing aging F-7 aircraft. This development occurs as China's defense exports face a downturn, with Asia being a primary market.

title: Israel, US parting ways? Netanyahu says Iran war 'not over', seeks arms independence
content: Speaking during a meeting with reserve combat officers in Gush Etzion in the occupied West Bank, Netanyahu stressed the need for Israel to strengthen its domestic defence industry despite continued support from Washington. "I greatly appreciate the support we have received - and that I have secured over the years -- from our American friends. But today I say, we need our own independent weapons-production system. We must manufacture our own armaments."

title: 'My mom had to quit her job': Shedge makes emotional admission after maiden India call-up
content: Mumbai and Punjab Kings all-rounder Suryansh Shedge is thrilled after earning his maiden India call-up as Nitish Kumar Reddy's replacement for the T20I series against Ireland and England. The 23-year-old credited his parents and coaches for his success, praised captain Shreyas Iyer's leadership, and said he remains focused on enjoying cricket rather than having expectations.

title: Why Kylian Mbappe's France looks truly terrifying
content: Day 12 saw Dictator Kylian Mbappé turn France into a weapon of mass destruction, Lionel Messi keep chasing the Golden Boot, Erling Haaland drag Norway towards a blockbuster showdown, and the Adidas Trionda make goalkeepers question their life choices. Also, Ronaldo’s Portugal seek redemption on Day 13. Read this and more in today’s Offside.

title: US stocks slide as IT sell-off deepens, Nasdaq trims over 2%, Dow Jones falls 750 points
content: Global markets experienced a significant downturn Tuesday, with Wall Street indices plunging amid a deepening tech stock sell-off. Concerns over heavy AI spending and anticipated US interest rate hikes are fueling investor anxiety. Major tech giants and chipmakers saw substantial drops, mirroring weakness seen across Asian and European exchanges. Oil prices remained subdued following a US decision on Iranian oil sanctions.

title: 'How can you hate Vaibhav Sooryavanshi?': Ashwin explodes at critics
content: Veteran spinner Ravichandran Ashwin has come to the defense of young cricketer Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, urging against excessive criticism following an on-field incident. Ashwin emphasized the need to differentiate between constructive feedback and personal attacks, highlighting that Sooryavanshi is still developing. He encouraged allowing the talented youngster to play freely, free from undue pressure, as he prepares for a potential India debut.

title: PM Modi calls on President Droupadi Murmu amid Cabinet reshuffle buzz
content: Speculation is rife about a Union Cabinet reshuffle following Prime Minister Narendra Modi's meeting with President Droupadi Murmu. Recent appointments of Union ministers to key BJP organizational roles, coupled with the resignation of George Kurian and the non-renomination of Ravneet Singh to the Rajya Sabha, have intensified these discussions. These developments suggest potential changes within the government's ministerial ranks.

title: Rohit Sharma conferred Padma Shri for outstanding contribution to Indian cricket
content: Cricket icon Rohit Sharma has been honored with the prestigious Padma Shri for his significant contributions to Indian cricket. President Droupadi Murmu presented the award, recognizing Sharma's stellar career as a batsman and captain. His leadership guided India to the 2024 T20 World Cup and subsequent Champions Trophy glory, solidifying his legacy as one of the nation's most accomplished cricketers.

title: Psychology tells why parents never stop listening for their child's voice in crowded room
content: Ever mistaken a stranger's voice for your child's? Neuroscience explains this common phenomenon. Our brains are wired to instantly recognize familiar voices, especially those linked to strong emotional memories from infancy. This deep-seated auditory memory, honed by early caregiver bonding, allows for rapid identification even in noisy crowds, showcasing the remarkable power of our memory and emotional connections.

title: Why the Middle East still beats all other options in India’s energy basket
content: India's energy sector is closely watching the Strait of Hormuz situation, highlighting a continued reliance on the Middle East for crucial LPG supplies. While alternative sources like the US were tapped during disruptions, they couldn't fully bridge the gap. Higher LNG prices also impacted demand, with a return to more favorable rates expected to boost consumption. This crisis underscores India's energy sourcing vulnerabilities.

title: Registering property online: What NGDRS does and how it works
content: Property registration in India is undergoing a digital transformation with the National Generic Document Registration System (NGDRS). This government platform allows citizens to prepare documents, assess property value, calculate stamp duty, and book appointments online. NGDRS aims to streamline the entire process, making it faster and more transparent, reducing the need for extensive paperwork and multiple visits to government offices.

title: President Murmu confers Padma awards: Shibu Soren, Alka Yagnik, Mammootty among honourees
content: President Droupadi Murmu presented Padma Awards at Rashtrapati Bhavan, honouring 65 distinguished individuals. The ceremony recognized achievements across various fields, including arts, sports, and medicine. Notable recipients included actor Mammootty, singer Alka Yagnik, and cricketer Rohit Sharma. Dr. Padma Gurmet, awarded for his work in preserving Himalayan medical traditions, expressed immense pride for his community.

title: R Madhavan conferred with Padma Shri, bows down to PM Narendra Modi
content: President Murmu bestowed the remaining Padma Awards 2026, including Padma Bhushan for Mammootty and Alka Yagnik, at Rashtrapati Bhavan. Prime Minister Modi and other dignitaries attended the ceremony where 65 honours were presented. Notable recipients also included R Madhavan and Rohit Sharma, while veteran actor Satish Shah was honoured posthumously. This follows an earlier ceremony where Dharmendra received a posthumous Padma Vibhushan.

title: Pune businessman’s son pushed into gorge: How fiancée tried to pass it off as 'accident'
content: A trek to Lohagad Fort, initially thought to be a tragic accident, has been revealed as a murder. Police arrested a woman and her alleged lover for allegedly pushing her fiancé into a deep gorge. The victim, Ketan Vishal Agrawal, was lured to the fort under the guise of birthday celebrations. Investigators uncovered a conspiracy driven by the woman's alleged unwillingness to marry and her relationship with another man.

title: Trump waives sanctions on Iran crude oil: What it means for India
content: As part of a broader package designed to provide economic relief to Iran, the US Treasury announced a sanctions waiver valid until August 21. The exemption allows Tehran to export oil and related products and receive payments for those sales.

title: Trouble mounts for Telegram: Govt report flags concern over child abuse, fraud
content: During the legal proceedings, Telegram faced accusations of failing to adequately curb misinformation spread through its channels, allegations the company has rejected. While the ban was lifted on Tuesday, users will remain unable to access the platform's message-editing feature until June 30. India is Telegram's largest market with more than 150 million users.

title: Mammootty honoured with Padma Bhushan: President confers award, family joins ceremony
content: Veteran actor Mammootty was honored with the Padma Bhushan, India's third-highest civilian award, by President Droupadi Murmu. The prestigious ceremony, held at Rashtrapati Bhavan, saw his wife Sulfath and son Dulquer Salmaan by his side, celebrating his five decades of remarkable contributions to Indian cinema. This recognition underscores his enduring legacy and widespread impact across multiple film industries.

title: SIT inspects Lucknow fire incident site; Congress alleges Uttar Pradesh government’s negligence responsible for tragedy
content: SIT members Amrit Abhijat, Additional Chief Secretary, Tourism and Culture Department, and Praveen Kumar, ADG, Lucknow Zone, conducted a detailed inspection of the entire building for over an hour in the morning; a team from the Uttar Pradesh Forensic Science Laboratory (FSL) also collected crucial evidence from the site

title: CWMA asks basin States to use reservoir water judiciously, defers decision on Cauvery releases
content: The direction came at the 52nd meeting of the Cauvery Water Management Authority, held amid renewed political debate over the Mekedatu balancing reservoir project

title: U.S. marks Freedom 250 in Hyderabad, road near Consulate renamed ‘Donald Trump Avenue’
content: The designation of name on the road by the Telangana government, was described as a tribute to the United States and an acknowledgement of Hyderabad’s increasing importance in the U.S.-India relations

title: Guarantee schemes will continue but benefits only for eligible, says Revanna
content: Guarantees panel chief says that the authority has launched crackdown on misuse of schemes and is recovering wrongly transferred funds

title: SIT grills suspended CI Nagaraju in Sai Krishna case
content: Investigation officers also question Task Force police of NTR Commissionerate, who reportedly picked up the victim from Markapuram

title: Watch: Pune fort murder: Police allege Fiancée, lover pushed man into gorge
content: What was first reported as an accidental fall at Pune’s Lohagad Fort has now turned into a murder investigation

title: Watch: Lucknow fire tragedy: FIR reveals major safety lapses, 4 arrested
content: An FIR in the deadly Aliganj building fire case has alleged serious fire safety violations, lack of emergency exits, and unsafe electrical arrangements

title: Congress seeks high-level probe in alleged Ujjain land scam by Mohan Yadav family
content: Leader of Opposition in the Madhya Pradesh Legislative Assembly said that the land prices were increased in a “well planned manner” by bringing projects there, and land was purchased in those areas before announcing the projects.

title: Centre tightens FCRA rules for NGOs
content: NGOs must stick to specified activities in their category, geographical areas; disclose social media accounts, websites, publications; political content barred; separate fee charged for each category, State/UT where an NGO operates

title: Farmers protest in Dharwad against power supply privatisation move
content: Scores of Raita Sangha members take to the streets and block road in Dharwad seeking the fulfilment of various pending demands

title: Analysis: Stretching ‘information’ to include Telegram
content: By reading entire platforms run by companies, which were legal entities by their own nature, under the ambit of ‘information’ under the Act, the government has effectively weaponised the content-blocking provision of Section 69A

title: Thrissur MLA seeks link bypass, metro extension and Thrissur Package in Budget debate
content: Rajan Pallan calls for integrated urban planning, tourism circuit and financial relief for Thrissur Corporation

title: Plays to be staged in chamber format to mark ‘Crazy’ Mohan’s birth anniversary
content: Actor Maadhu Balaji says the idea to stage plays in a chamber format came from discussions with friends on performing for smaller audiences and staging shows specifically for them

title: Hyderabad Metro Rail take over and expansion talks in Delhi yield positive outcome; SBICAPS to act as consultant
content: CM A. Revanth Reddy convinces Union Ministers Ashwini Vaishnaw and Manoharlal Khattar about the Telangana government’s decision to take over the project and propose its expansion

title: A.P. Cabinet clears ₹34,000 cr. proposals approved by Investment Promotion Board
content: More than 80 agenda items receive approval, with major focus on attracting investments across industries, information technology, tourism, renewable energy, data centres and capital city development

title: Kerala reports surge in dengue, influenza, leptospirosis cases
content: 159 confirmed dengue cases reported in a single day in State; no let up in shigellosis cases either, with 15 cases reported from five districts

title: Naidu to inaugurate new spillway gates at Tungabhadra Project on June 25
content: ₹51-crore restoration completed after 2024 flood damage; 33 new gates enhance dam safety and secure irrigation and drinking water supply across Andhra Pradesh, Karnataka and Telangana

title: Syama Prasad Mookerjee, profound thinker and a great nationalist: BJP chief
content: “The sacrifice and patriotism of Dr. Mookerjee ji, who dedicated his entire life to the resolve of ‘Nation First’, serve as a guiding light for all of us,” says Nitin Nabin

title: APSCPCR chief calls for CWC, DCPU in Markapuram district
content: During a two-day visit to Prakasam district, Vetukuri A.V.S. Surya Narayana Raju also identifies several lapses in child protection and directed for corrective steps

title: RJD slams Bihar CM Samrat Choudhary for calling Lalu Prasad ‘greatest villain’
content: The Opposition party says the BJP-led government is playing with the rhetoric of withdrawal of security cover to the RJD chief to hide its failure in protecting poor, women and children

title: Puttur, Srikalahasti transport offices excel in public perception feedback
content: Puttur unit, led by Motor Vehicle Inspector V. Chandrashekhar, records 100% positive public perception, while Motor Vehicle Inspector Damodar Naidu’s team in Srikalahasti registers a 96% positive public perception

title: 76% health insurance reimbursement claimants face immediate financial burden: report
content: Ability to manage costs only with liquid savings dropped from 32% to 24% in 2024-25 while financial stress faced by insurance holders at the time of raising claims has gone up across all tiers of cities between 2023 and 2025, says consumer insights report by online marketplace Policybazaar

title: Technical issues found with elevated road at Kittur Chennamma Circle
content: The slow pace of construction on the structure in Hubballi continues to trouble motorists and the general public

title: Fisheries and Food Safety depts. in Kerala launch ‘Operation Sagar Rani’ to ensure quality of fish
content: Officials urge consumers to examine fish carefully before purchase, check its firmness and colour of eyes to detect deterioration; surprise checks being held at harbours, boat landing centres, and online stores

title: Annamayya police arrest man for multiple thefts
content: Police seize 42 grams of gold jewellery worth ₹6 lakhs, 250 grams of silver articles worth ₹50,000, and a splendor plus two-wheeler from the accused

title: AAP MLA Chaitar Vasava, wife among nine sentenced to seven years in assault, extortion case
content: Describing the conviction as politically motivated, AAP Gujarat president Isudan Gadhvi said the party would challenge the verdict before a higher court

title: Karnataka High Court notice to State on plea challenging May 2026 notification on sub-classification of SC communities for reservation
content: The High Court of Karnataka ordered issue of notice to the State government on another petition by the Confederation of Untouchable Nomadic Communities of Karnataka and others challenging the government’s May 8, 2026 notification, which had revised the August 2025 notifications on sub-classification of Scheduled Castes communities into three groups and distribution of percentage of reservation

title: President confers Padma awards on Mammootty, Vijay Amritraj
content: Alka Yagnik, Mammootty, U.S.-based oncologist Dattatreyudu Nori, tennis player Vijay Amritraj, industrialist S.K.M. Maeilanandhan, and social worker Vellappally Natesan received Padma Bhushan from the President during the ceremony

title: Clear pending benefits under Bhovi Corporation within seven days, officials told
content: Corporation chairman reviews implementation of welfare schemes in Bidar, directs officials to expedite disposal of pending cases

title: Kadapa Collector urges farmers to choose horticultural crops
content: As part of ‘Rythanna Meekosam’, the weeklong farmer outreach programme conducted by the government,  Sreedhar Cherukuri visits various farms in Chennur, Khajipet and Kadapa rural mandals on Tuesday

title: A.P. reserves 33% quarry leases for Vaddera societies
content: Amended mining rules provide preferential allotment of select minor mineral leases, 50% fee concession and a dedicated quota to improve livelihood opportunities for the traditional quarrying community

title: To Carry Out Murder Plan, Fiancee Sabotaged Bali Trip Plan, Stole Passport
content: Siya Goyal, Ketan Agrawal's fiancee, and her lover, Chetan Babulal Choudhary, have been arrested for allegedly pushing the businessman into a deep gorge off Lohagad Fort in Pune and passing it off as an accident, officials said.

title: The Desert Shield: Why the UAE is Betting on India's BrahMos and Akashteer
content: The UAE has traditionally bought its weapons from Western countries. So, why are they suddenly so interested in Indian defence technology?

title: Explained: Why Petrol Prices in India Don't Follow Crude's Trajectory
content: Crude oil prices that shot up amid the Middle East war has started to return to their pre-war levels.

title: Fiancee's Failed Attempt 4 Days Before Murder, A Snake Excuse, And A Hug
content: Vishal Agarwal, father of the 26-year-old real estate director, has described how Siya pushed Ketan from the fort on June 14. When Ketan survived by grabbing a bush, Siya shouted that a snake was approaching and then hugged him.

title: Video: Truck Falls In River After Bridge Collapses In Himachal, Driver Safe
content: The bridge gave way when the 10-tire dumper truck loaded with sand was about to reach the other side. The vehicle was overloaded, officials said.

title: "Broom Warrior" To Geneticist: 6 Key Padma Shri Awardees Honoured Today
content: It marked the second civil investiture ceremony, where the President conferred 65 Padma Awards, including two Padma Vibhushan, seven Padma Bhushan and 56 Padma Shri.

title: Case Filed Against Bihar Police Officers In Bharat Tiwari Encounter
content: The case names the then Jagdishpur SDPO, the then Shahpur SHO and other police personnel associated with the incident, according to the statement.

title: Ukraine Has An "Army Of Witches". They Are Killing Russians At Will
content: Over four years, the war in Ukraine has changed, and with it, its people and tactics. It is now fought in the steady accumulation of small pieces of information that eventually become a set of coordinates and, shortly afterwards, a drone strike.

title: For New Borns In Tamil Nadu, Gold Rings From 'Uncle' Vijay
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay is set to launch the 'Thai Maman Gold Ring Scheme', under which a one-gram gold ring will be gifted to every child born in government hospitals across the state.

title: "B Team Of Disruptive Elements": Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan To NDTV On CJP
content: Those who were rejected in the democracy have come in disguise and are now after the system, said Dharmendra Pradhan, slamming the CJP.

title: Hormuz Ship Traffic Highest Since Start Of Middle East War: Report
content: At least 37 commodity carriers transited the Strait of Hormuz on Monday, a record level since the start of the Middle East war in late February, according to data from the maritime tracking firm Kpler.

title: "Left Me On My Birthday": Woman's Instagram Story After Killing Fiance
content: Siya shared an emotional Instagram story mourning his death. In the post, she expressed deep grief and posed questions about his sudden departure.

title: Iran, Oman Plan Talks on Hormuz Control, Service Charges
content: Oman and Iran will study the future administration of the Strait of Hormuz and the costs to be charged for services provided, they said in a joint statement Tuesday, insisting on sovereignty over the vital waterway.

title: Adani Mundra Airport Begins Commercial Ops, Inaugural Flight Lands From Goa
content: Earlier in the day, regional carrier Star Air became the first airline to start scheduled commercial flights at the airport, with the inaugural flight landing from Goa.

title: Eiffel Tower To Close Early Due To Severe Heatwave
content: The Eiffel Tower, one of the most visited tourist attractions in the world, was on Tuesday set to close early because of a severe heatwave, its operator said.

title: Birthday Bash On Assam Bridge Lands 14 In Legal Trouble, Luxury Cars Seized
content: A late-night birthday celebration held on the Kumar Bhaskar Varma Bridge has sparked legal action after videos of the event circulated widely on social media, drawing criticism over public safety and misuse of a vital transport corridor.

title: Delhi Colony Keeps Trash Out Of Landfills, Shows Way With 'Zero-Waste' Model
content: The model focuses on managing waste within the community instead of sending it directly to landfill sites.

title: "I Felt Jumping Was Only Option": Survivor Recounts Lucknow Fire Horror
content: By the time the door finally opened, thick smoke had begun filling the floor. The staircase -- their only escape route -- had already become a tunnel of smoke and flames.

title: Forgotten Warnings, Delayed Reforms: Why Coaching Hubs Are Vulnerable To Fire
content: An NDTV reality check in South Delhi's Kalu Sarai and Neb Sarai - two of the capital's largest coaching centre clusters - suggests that many of the same safety concerns continue to exist despite repeated warnings, inquiries and promises of reform.

title: Tarique Rahman Begins China Visit, Teesta Project Talks On The Agenda
content: On 24 June, Tarique Rahman will hold a meeting with the prime minister of Kazakhstan and later depart Dalian for Beijing by high-speed train later in the day. Rahman will also meet leaders of other countries on the trip.

title: No salary slip, no day off: Homemakers ask if 24-hour labour can be valued at Rs 30,000
content: Aastha Jha is a writer at The Times of India, covering politics. She also writes on business, global affairs, social issues, with a focus on policy, governance and their impact on everyday lives. Her work brings together reportage and analysis, tracking developments in India and abroad while examining the broader social and economic currents shaping public discourse. Read More

title: India, China, Iraq: How Iran's frozen billions across the world are trapped in a sanctions maze
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock.
